reviews.review_text: For all the linux friends, this game works perfectly under proton! No crashes, no stuttering, just smooth graphics and wonderful gameplay. Praise the omnissiah!
People with over 1000 hours leaving negative reviews just because of 2 capes are weird.

- get home after stressful day at work
- fire up the game
- burn some heretics
- bath in the blood of alien filth
- spam "FOR THE EMPEROR" whenever you travel on the elevator, also offer a handshake to your battle brothers
- sleep like a baby
- profit
Legendary game. Graphics, lore, main characters, PvE, PvP, gorgeous customization options. Gameplaywise it's like Gears of War but better in my opinion.

Requiring an online connection for all unlocks and progression is plain BS.  If you have even the tiniest interruption in internet for whatever reason, and the game disconnects you and shoves you into offline mode.  None of your progress in offline mode counts for any unlocks and gets erased once you go back to the title screen to reconnect or restart the game.  That's right, there is no auto-reconnect or option to retry connection, so you can't preserve any of your progress if you happen to get disconnected for even just a split second.  I have lost over 20 hours of progress thanks to this.  I don't usually post reviews, but this was so frustrating that it warranted one.

[h1]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/h1]

[i]The Fellowship of the Bolter[/i]

[b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] is as straightforward as a game can get, in the same way pulp science fiction often is: its hero Captain Titus, returning to the battlefield, leading his fellow Ultramarines against the endless tide of Tyranids. It’s a war story told in very broad strokes, you've got your heroism, sacrifice, and the sheer spectacle of humanity’s armored champions holding back the swarm.

[b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] is part of a lineage that stretches back to the late 1980's fascination with grimdark universes and power fantasies. [b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] is a 3rd person shooter that ditches competitive deathmatches or cover mechanics and goes for jolly cooperation instead, leaning hard on the camaraderie of three players fighting side by side. While it might not redefine the co‑op action genre, what is here is a blisteringly good time when played with other mates as it attempts to marry the operatic scale of [b]Warhammer[/b] with the accessibility of modern gameplay design.

Saber Interactive's craftmanship here is solid. In [b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] they continue to build on the amazing Swarm engine displayed in [b]World War Z[/b], an engine that is able to impressively render battlefields teeming with enemies, while the games systems of shooting, melee, and hacking drive the moment‑to‑moment gameplay into really satisfying rhythms. Co‑op is clearly the crown jewel here and in my opinion the way the game should be played. Missions are designed and structured in such a way that they oftentimes demand teamwork, with each character’s abilities complementing the others. Don't fret however if co-op is not your jam as single‑player still remains enjoyable; the AI companions are competent enough to keep the experience from feeling hollow thankfully. Fidelity of execution is not flawless as animations can be stiff, and repetition often does creep in, but the game’s ambition is clear, it wants you to have bloody good fun while hacking, shooting and ripping things apart.

What [b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] really nails is: that it really is more fun when played with others. The laughter, the shared panic, the triumphant relief when a swarm is finally beaten back, these are the moments that elevate it. And yet, for those who prefer solitude, the campaign still delivers a satisfying power fantasy. The games main campaign offers about 12 missions with another 12 missions specifically designed for co-op featuring a B-Cast of characters you hear about through the main games campaign. Truth be told, my co-op partner and myself found we both enjoyed the side co-op missions way more than the base games, although the base games missions are no slouch either, it's just we enjoyed the co-op ones more.

As a gamer I always like my reviews to pose the question of whether the game is worth the price of admission, and more importantly, the demand on your time? Yes. [b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine 2[/b] offers enough content and replay value to justify the investment of both money and time. It is not a masterpiece, but it is a sturdy, entertaining entry in the [b]Warhammer[/b] canon, and a reminder that sometimes the joy of games lies not in perfection, but in the shared chaos of battle. 

[h1]One word or phrase to sum up the experience[/h1]
[i]Glorious chaos, best shared.[/i] 

[h1]Performance/Bug related incidents[/h1]
[i]None the game ran flawlessly for the most part. There was only one mission that bugged out putting my character into first person mode but a quick restart of the mission resolved it.[/i]

[h1]Similar to games[/h1]
[i][b]Warhammer 4K: Space Marine[/b], [b]World War Z[/b], [b]Back for Blood[/b][/i]

[u][b]Rating[/b][/u]
★★★★★★★☆☆☆

i ran this game before i switched from a gtx 1650 super, it looked like garbage but it was stable, it looks good now and runs even better, wish we could get a necron expansion

the game is super fun esp if you love warhammer 40k but the fckn size of this game is insane. it worse than a call of duty title taking up so much space its super annoying. with every update the game file gets bigger and bigger. and they charge waaaayy too much for simple cosmetics that should be in the game its basically a quick cash grab. if the file size dosnt piss you off the price of simple cosmetics will and if that dont piss you off the balancing in the game will. the story is great though i love it im still stuck on the last boss its very difficult on the hardest difficulty setting. the online is fun when you can get kills and itrs not some sweats in game.

I love this game, as it gives me and a lot of other players the power fantasy (more or less) of a space marine. But ive never seen such disrespect of someones time in these new ordeals. A HUNDRED absolute difficulty missions for every single class (600 total missions) for some capes, hell, its not even for every single class as sniper gets count em additional TWO ANTENNA for your pack, whilst bulwark a couple generic iron halos? 

I get what they were going for, "oh its a challenging thing but youll get some cool new accessories" but did they even realise what they were asking their community to do for these pieces? did whoever QA these ordeals (if at all) even attempt to try to grind out for one of these themselves? For those who intend to get these, god speed to you all and I hope your random players dont throw.

Unless you're ready to slog through hundreds of the hardest missions in the game, I would steer clear for those who want to customize, as you will find out quickly a lot of the things you want are locked behind tens if not hundreds of hours of grind.

It pains me to not recommend this game in it's current state, as I'm very passionate about the game and 40K in general. However after the recent update (13), I feel the goal-posts have been moved. 
The higher difficulties, though challenging, were still doable and enjoyable prior to the update. This has changed with the new update, they've ramped the difficulty up, essentially making absolute the equivalent of what siege was and siege a whole different beast with added modifiers. In my opinion the siege modifiers should be optional similar to how stratagems work. 
Furthermore with the added capes, which have been long requested, the devs have decided to make them extremely hard to obtain. They've since reduced the requirements but it still feels over the top for some basic customisation. I get the extreme players want something to show off, but it seems most of the player base won't have the time/skill to ever wear one. (unless it's the paid DLC capes of course)

It feels like the added changes almost gatekeep newer/casual players from even reaching the higher difficulties, enjoying the game at that level of pure chaos which was fantastic and do-able pre update. Just seems like a really strange decision in my humble opinion and somewhat counter productive.

Other than these irritating changes, which has really put me off continuing playing, as the new stuff now feels unobtainable, the game is honestly fantastic and is (was) a great deal of fun. The last update has just left a rather bitter taste in my mouth as I'm sick to death of running the same missions/stratagems and getting no rewards as I unfortunately have a job and cannot sink 1000's of hours into the game like the basement dwellers this update feels aimed towards. (Lucky basement dwellers not having to work)
Here's hoping they will ease the difficulty and rebalance some things, as to which my review will change into a glowing one as it was pre update 13.
Glad I waited to get this on sale. Too repetitive relying on the same 3 mechanics over and over. Dodge, parry, execute, and repeat.  No mission feels any different than the last.
Pure, addictive chaos. If you want to fulfill your fantasy of becoming an unstoppable, full-metal beast and obliterating everything in sight, this is it. 100% recommended.

a couple weeks ago i would've recommended this game to everyone i could but now im gonna tell you to not waste a single dollar on it, patch 13 ruined the game completely, its not fun anymore, its not even slightly enjoyable, especially with the frame drops that came with patch 13, it sickens me because this was genuinely my favorite game
( i dont have the greatest pc but i now cant even make it past wave 15 because it would be physically unplayable, before i was making it to wave 25 or so with no issues )

I had a really good time with Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2, especially playing it together with a friend. The co-op experience is a lot of fun and makes every mission feel even more intense. What I enjoyed the most was the melee combat, slashing through hordes of Tyranids feels incredibly satisfying and never really gets old. The game does a great job of making you feel powerful while still throwing enough enemies at you to keep things exciting. The visuals, atmosphere, and overall Warhammer 40K feeling are excellent, and it's easy to get immersed in the constant action. While there are a few small issues here and there, they never took away from the fun for me. If you're a fan of Warhammer 40K or just enjoy action packed co-op games with satisfying combat, this is definitely worth checking out. 
Highly recommended!❤️‍🔥

I'm late to the party, but it's still a rager. People say this game reminds them of the 360/PS3 days and I get it. It doesn't take itself super seriously, though it does take it's source content seriously, it's not trying to change your life and say something about society, it's just a fun time blasting aliens and traitors in a grim dark diesel-punk universe. Hlaf shooter, half beat 'em up, and all fun.

The community is pretty cool too.

First i loved 40k Space Marine 1 on Xbox360 long ago.

Unable to play the Campaign, Shameful Online requirement for solo. Bought it full price to be spit on.
I've tried so many times offline since launch but it doesn't work because of online enemies build.
The game is not even too realistic to be unplayable. It's not even funny for fans.

[+] Dubbing are good.
[-] Vomiting Woke content.
⭐2/5⭐

Apparently 16GB of VRAM is not enough to play this game without it being crashed, you want to turn on frame gen? you get less FPS, you want to turn on Ray Tracing? it won't run, and there is a delay when you dodge which is extremely annoying as it can be the game decider which cost me several matches, mind you this happens a lot actually, you perfectly dodge but the game won't register the input, as a result many matches were lost for no fault of mine, wish it was better but yeah it is what it is.
Do not enjoy the new update changes. The new items and cosmetics are nice, but I hate the new game play changes.

fun game but the playerbase is extremely low quality, feels like they're playing this purely because it's warhammer and not because they are actually good at this kind of game. so many mastery 4 level 25 players will complain about bugs, complain about not being revived, complain about "ughhhh this modifier sucks!!!!" all while you're fighting off a whole wave alone but the moment you try telling them that maybe, just maybe, they should take on challenges appropriate to their skill level or at least keep their mouth shut and not expect you to dedicate your entire existence to keeping them alive, you become the problem because boo hoo that's toxic. it's a game we're all here to have fun!!!! tell me where's the fun in watching your max level vanguard dive into the very first group of enemies that spawned on the map and instantly die because wait what it's a fallen vanguard mission?? tell me where's the fun in watching your max level bulwark die 1v1 to a chaos spawn because bruh no contested hp is terrible!! tell me where's the fun in watching your max level heavy just stand and scratch his forehead as you kite around a group of minoris enemies because he's busy receiving signals from the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mothership regarding what his next action should be.

the lack of a lobby browser makes the multiplayer part feel like a rudiment. often times you'll join a mission right before the final encounter, which some people might welcome if they're just grinding, but it becomes a problem when you're trying to claim your weekly accolades from the siege mode because it's almost impossible to find a lobby that doesn't have an AFK host, a level 1 player who randomly decided that their first mission absolutely has to be one of the highest difficulty ones, the exact same AFK host from before because would you look at this he has a spot on his squad just for you! OR one that's not already in progress because you HAVE to be present for all the 15 waves in order to claim the reward, otherwise you have to do extra waves beyond the 15th which the squad you just joined is most likely and understandably not on board with.

there is a number of character and weapon perks in this game that provide powerful effects but have a cooldown between activations. that's all well and good but for whatever asinine reason the developers are either too lazy or just incapable of adding a HUD element that actually tells you how much of the cooldown is left so if you have any of them equipped you just have to GUESS what you're capable of at any given moment in time unless your secret talent that you only tell your closest friends about is an internal clock with an accuracy that rivals even the atomic ones that take like a million years to start deviating by a milisecond (numbers made up for emphasis)

just in general so many bottom of the barrel players regardless of level force themselves into the highest difficulty endgame activities when they don't even know which end of the gun should be pointed towards the enemy. while doing this week's siege I, first of all, spent more time looking at loading screens than actually playing the friggin mission (see paragraph 2). and when i finally got into a lobby that just started and had 2 max level players i thought now we can finally chill and break a few xenos skulls except playing with those two felt more like punishment than the actual enemy waves.  they spent all their siege currency (the blue stuff idk what it's called) buying guardian relics (the self-revive item) and at the end had 10+ incapacitations each. if i played that any worse we'd literally have no bullets to shoot with because the only things they bought were to keep themselves alive so i had to cover for both myself and them. all the time they were emoting me like i'm some sort of dance monkey meant to serve and carry them. there really needs to be some sort of merit system to restrict access to endgame activities to people who, realistically, should be playing with letter blocks and not even be allowed to use a computer for anything, because, as it stands right now, the already terrible bare bones matchmaking system is being polluted by completely inept individuals who nonetheless feel entitled to being carried because they want to get heroic weapons as fast as possible and it is your job as the wholesome chungus marine to make their dream come true no matter what.

Horrible performances, Horrible enemy balancing, and Horrible overpriced season passes.

I cannot even like the game without Saber even knowing how to balance certain enemies and their combat system being so janky and iffy when it comes to hitboxes (Zoanthrope beams). Even when I try to learn every enemies attack it's just straight up an inch of an enemy hitting me and somehow I get rolled to the floor like a toddler being thrown around like a football.
Enraged status is literally the stupidest idea mainly on "Ranged" enemies because they already do a lot of damage  and even if you're constantly trying to find cover n dodge they would still shred you regardless or not. The games still only about to be 2 years old so we can't really see any further changes I bet until the games feeling more n more polished over the more patches we go.
Stun locking is also a main issue with this game where if a enemy target "Super Heavy Hits" you it cannot be avoided once you're on the ground trying to get up because the animation is slow and then you get followed up by another enemy who is preparing an attack on you when you don't even have enough animation time to get back up n counterplay.
The difficulty scaling is pretty much the worst I've ever seen in games where increasing enemy dmg while also decreasing our health pools are not a great mix when if you're already increasing dmg then what is the point of even trying to decrease hp?
The contested health and armour shield system is completely useless in this game but I find the contested health to be more viable but we lack too many options to get health back unless you either have a weapon of high dmg to get it back instantly or a good amount, I personally wish the armour shield system was like in Darktide where teams can stick together to increase the regen speed of armour to also incorporate more teamplay.

I wanna like the game but if Saber still decides to not properly balance stuff then I might just think about quitting and shelving the game. I wanna learn to play the game to where I can at least stand my ground to the higher difficulties but I just cannot withstand the amount of crappy hitbox coding they've got going.

[h1]NOT RECOMMENDED[/h1]

Essentially a 95% L4D like coop, PvE, PvP game with a 5% short story mode like what EA does
The story is too short and boring, it baits then goes nowhere, uninspired characters designed to just tick boxes
Melee combat and audio is terrible, impact feedback is dull, feels like cutting through butter, gun sounds are fine,
Cannot change your marine faces, which is bs when you can change their chapters,

Why I never bought at launch full price and waited for sale but even after 67% off felt like a waste of money and time now unlike the first game back then but GW has also derailed since then, deepest sale or dont bother

Just bought this in May 26.

What a shower. In about 4 hours of play I've had half a dozen crashes to desktop. Had to reduce res to get past cutscene at the end of the first mission.

Funny thing is I found the solution posted on a forum 9 months ago, The fact that this still hasn't been fixed is shameful.  In addition, my game is constantly telling me that mods are detected. So I couldn't play online if I wanted to.

Thank God I only paid £16 as if I'd been confronted with this at launch for a £55 game I'd be fuming. I doubt I will spend any more time trying to play it as it's just a buggy mess.

It's great if you like running the same 4 operations thousands of times for the next piece of drip-fed content locked behind your wallet or even more mind-numbing grinding. Nothing in this game was thought out and the devs have been slowly (emphasis on SLOWLY) trying to patch up the many, many holes in this game's design. And yet it gets a pass from people who are used to getting bent over by Games Workshop just because it's dressed up in some Space Marine armor. Don't make the same mistake as I did. Respect your own time and give this game a pass because the devs certainly don't.

Space Marine 2 is generally above average at best, and only thanks to the Art and Story (Which leans heavily on you having played the first Space Marine to understand fully the plot points etc happening.)

The Combat, which should feel visceral, brutal and awesome... generally doesn't feel like that at all, with weapons feeling absurdly weak, with every melee weapon requiring multiple (2-3) hits to kill even basic 'Gaunts (including power weaponry, like power fist light attacks which in lore is an anti-armor weapon that should obliterate weak infantry with a single hit but struggle against multiple opponents) to ranged weaponry feeling horrendously weak too, like Bolters requiring a full on mag dump to larger (Majoris+) enemies heads to put them into execute state, or Plasma rifle/pistol requiring 4-5 overcharged shots to put them into Execute state, using 10 Ammo (8 or less) per shot to do so. And yes, even with Relic Tier weapons with all perks for said weapon unlocked the weapons feel unbelievably weak, with almost all of them effectively forcing you to only land headshots as otherwise you will not be able to kill enemies quickly enough before they force you into a melee rigamarole where said melee strikes dont do enough damage at all.

Melee Weapons generally being horrendously weak (Besides for charged power fist attacks) Generally forces you into the parry mechanic rather than guiding you to it, as if you dont use Gunstrikes or Block's damage increase constantly you will be doing so little damage that it will require, depending on the difficulty, up to a minute or more of just hitting the same enemy nonstop over and over again even with weapon tiers on appropriate difficulties.

The PVE Gamemode, Operations, quickly becomes very boring because you're stuck replaying the same 13 missions over and over again to level up 7 classes 5 times due to the prestige system, which is also terrible because after every level reset, you end up using the same perks over and over again until you've unlocked the last Prestige, at which point you dont really have a reason to touch the class anymore besides going into Lethal or Absolute Difficulties.

Generally speaking there isn't actual difficulty in the game either, as the moment you've "Mastered" the Parry and dodge mechanics, enemies will be unable to hit you besides for chip damage sometimes which you can armor restore anyway. Not to mention that each difficulty level simply increases enemy health, damage and their overall numbers, while decreasing your stats accordingly, making you even weaker.

Space Marine 1, which came out 13 years prior to this, has a far more simpler but considerably more robust combat system, with no invulnerability frames to punish you just spamming executes to regain health, to an armor system that rewards you for both hit and run and targeting heavier hitting enemies as fast as possible that also synergizes well with the execute system to help you more safely regain health.

Space Marine 2's combat system is far too complicated while being somehow too simple at the same time, railroading you most of the time into a melee-focused parry gamestyle even on classes that are stronger in range (unless you choose specific perks obviously). Armor being regained from executes and from lethal (Or non-lethal with certain perks) Gun-strikes forces you to constantly be in combat, especially with the difficulties increasing armor regen delay from 25s all the way up to 75s, While Health has the Contested Health Point system which is tragically tedious rather than actually increasing difficulty of the game, as you eventually start to rely solely on perks that restore your health because health restoration items are far too rare in contrast to the amount of damage you can receive with even one mistake in combat, as even though most classes have 3 armor bars, Any "medium" Sized enemy (Like Tyranid warrior or Chaos Spawn) Will do 2 bars of damage on every difficulty, with projectiles flying everywhere and the horde enemies consistently hitting you near-nonstop.

The CHP System is also considerably annoying simply because of the way its designed, as the moment you lose health and gain CHP, you have less than 2s to hit an enemy as hard as possible to recover all (or almost all) of it, or you suffer chip damage. Certain perks do increase the time window, but start feeling mandatory to reduce the amount of permanent* punishment you receive. (* that you can only restore with high-level perks or stims)

Another factor is enemies staggering you to an insane degree where you can genuinely start bouncing from enemy to enemy like a ping pong ball (It has happened less and less for me as I've learned how to play the game, but it is still frustrating and annoying rather than feeling like I've made a legitimate mistake), while you are actively discouraged from generally staggering enemies, as that prevents you from parrying which results in you doing considerably less damage. Although you can stagger enemies, its simply "reserved" for specific situations like stopping an enemy from calling aid. And again, while you do have perks that can REDUCE the amount of stagger you receive, not only are they usually locked behind being high level (Like bulwark lvl 25), they feel basically mandatory for newer players who want to learn the game but basically are unable to use said perks because by the point they've unlocked them, they're already resetting to gain prestige perks.

TL;DR: Story and art are fine, but the rest of the game is very lackluster, frustrating and tedious.

Also game runs smoothly enough even with my PC, which is another positive.

(EDIT) Forgot to add, the first Space Marine was considerably less frustrating even on the hardest difficulty where certain enemies can 2-3 hit you to death, while in Space Marine 2 the "Intended" difficulty of Veteran felt like a slog at times.

This is a return to a simpler era in video games: you fight a wave of enemies, press a button, walk a little, and the process repeats. What makes it work is the incredibly satisfying combat. The mix of shooting and melee is simply brilliant, and the graphics are phenomenal. The sound is very immersive and makes every finisher even more satisfying, especially when you get into the rhythm of the game, with perfectly timed parries and dodges.

Graphically, this game is a delight. The enemies look phenomenal, with scenarios where you're outnumbered by the dozens. If you're a Warhammer 40k fan, you'll love it. The campaign continues years after the first game with the return of the beloved Lieutenant Demetrian Titus, reinstated as a Space Marine in the 2nd Company of the Ultramarines, in an effort to repel a Tyranid invasion (a mix between insect and dinosaur).

The online component is another area where the game shines. The Operations mode, playable online or solo, expands the campaign with mini-stories featuring other Ultramarines squads fighting to repel the Tyranids and Chaos agents. Selecting from seven different classes in teams of three, you can customize your marine to your liking, offering a nod to the hobby of assembling and painting miniatures.

PvP is also quite good, giving us a glimpse into the Horus Heresy (Loyalist agains Heretics). It pits Astartes against each other in fast-paced and entertaining skirmishes, using the same classes as the Operations mode. While it's not groundbreaking online, it's quite fun.

Ultimately, it's an excellent introduction to the Grimdark universe of Warhammer 40k, and if you're already a fan, you'll enjoy it even more.

I enjoyed the single player campaign.  But everything else about this game has been awful for me.  The game has crazy excessive load times.  The multiplayer is miserable.  There's no team mechanics and the marines are so squishy.  There's not really a way to heal or get ammo so you and your 'allies' fight over supplies like rats in a dumpster.  Get it on-sale for the campaign and then don't bother with the multiplayer skirmishes.

Cool movie to watch, meh game to play. 

Gameplay is kinda boring as a solo
Looks good, pretty fun. Doesn't take too long to complete. Endless extended game available if you wish

AWFUL. 

I had luck since last patch. No lag, no high ping for no reason and my gun shooted just fine.

Now. All above. Tried to play for 2 hour straight, couldn't make one game. My gun don't shoot for no reason at some point, high ping... in solo.. And hell, ennemy just hit through parry and dodge.

Game is designed to be played coop. if you want to play solo you are LOCKED with 2 bots with questionable programming - either they melt everyone around you or fluoride stare the objective being swarmed.

The combat gets repetitive in less than 30 minutes. The graphics are very good, soundstrack is mid, combat is 70% mashing righclick because you have no idea wtf is going on due to all the visual clutter. 

And if you play solo you dont have to worry that much about being downed since your unkillable bots will come and ress you. At certain times the bots even play the game for you, just sit back watch them kill everything.

Overall im glad I got this on a pretty good discount cuz if I bought this full price I would be livid. Ill try to finish it eventually but good god is the gameplay boring ( only 2 hours in btw).
nearly an hour of playtime and it's just been me waiting for the game to not freeze while launching

I love this game dont get me wrong, siege mode has always been my favorite gamemode in the game since it released. However, hormagaunts tanking half a mag of the heavy bolter is not what i would consider fun lol. Make the modifiers and scaling difficulty optional for Siege Mode!

Let's just get this out of the way. Is Space Marine 2 a good game by modern standards? Yes. Very much so. But modern standards have been down the toilet for a long time. Compared to its predecessor or any other third person shooter from the era of the original Space Marine, SM2 is a mediocre at best horde shooter that grows boring incredibly quickly, unless you're starved for entertainment or have friends to play with. Emphasis on friends, because randoms are, as usual with these kind of games, either complete pants-on-head or tryhards, with zero inbetween. 

SM1, you felt like a space marine. You were huge compared to the average human, but you didn't feel like it. You felt like this transhuman supersoldier, wading into the worst battlefields whilst shoota rounds plinked off your power armour. SM2, you feel your size. You're big, you're bulky, and you can't maneuver like a real space marine. In SM1, you hit like a truck, but you had speed on your side, as well. Swinging a chainsword felt like swinging a sword. In SM2, swinging a chainsword feels like swinging a chainsaw underwater. It's a miracle you can even hit anything. Add to that the unnecessary inclusion of parrying, because we've got to copy the Soulsborne formula in everything, apparently, and you've got a boring combat system that really doesn't sell the power fantasy.

Enemies, we get worse. SM1, you had Orks and you had Chaos. Orks rushed you (unless they were shoota boyz), Chaos shot at you (unless they were bloodletters). Nice. Simple. SM2, you have Tyranids and Thousand Sons. The difference? Thousand Sons teleport away from you when you try to kill them. That's the only difference. Both of them are full of swarmers (gaunts and tzaangors), both of them have the same elites under different names (warrior=rubric marine, lictor/ravener=scarab occult, zoanthrope=sorcerer, carnifex=spawn). Now, Tyranids being swarmers makes sense. They've always been about swarming, going right back to their first appearance in WH40K the actual tabletop game. But Thousand Sons are just Chaos with more psykers. Why are they behaving like Tyranids? And where are the daemons? SM1 had bloodletters, despite the Chaos faction (the Chosen of Nemeroth) being Undivided, but there's nothing for SM2's poster boys of Tzeentch? And no, the Lord of Change doesn't count. That's a set-piece, not an enemy.

Adding to the above, teleporters. In SM1, the only teleporters were bloodletters, who would use it to get closer to you so they could do what Khorne demands, i.e. rip and tear. In SM2, you've got raveners diving into their burrow and then coming out somewhere else. That's fine, that's acceptable, it's how they behave, it makes sense. But then, you've got rubrics and scarab occult, especially the former, who just can't sit still. You can't pin them down without luck. Every time you think you have, they teleport away again. Bad enemy design is bad enemy design, no matter how you try to dress it up, not that I'd know how Sabre tried to defend this decision. 

Campaign is a joke, for people who want to play solo. Half of it is locked behind the multiplayer "Operations" mode, which, even on the easiest difficulty, is balls to the wall hard if you have to rely on the bots, and only worth playing with friends. The story is basically just SM1, but with Tyranids instead of Orks and Thousand Sons instead of the Chosen of Nemeroth. Titus comes in, accompanied by not-Sidonus and not-Leandros, fights through hordes of invaders to get to the magical macguffin (the same Warp-based power source from the first game, because we couldn't do something original and because the Imperium is somehow stupid enough to see something tainted by Chaos and think "It'll be fine!"), power source is used, Chaos uses power source to open portals and invade, Titus must turn things around and save the day. Some would say you can't do much with a 40k story. To them, I point to the original, THQ SM2, where the storyline followed Titus's time in the Deathwatch after the events of SM1. Just because you can't do much besides "there is only war" in 40k, doesn't mean you have to rip the first game's storyline point for point. The only notable difference between the two is that SM2 teases the Necrons, and then proceeds to do absolutely nothing with them. Probably because there's no swarmers, meaning Sabre can't remake The Walking Dead with a 40k skin for a third time.

Multiplayer is a joke. On release, they didn't have half the game modes you had in the first game. Just team deathmatch and king of the hill. Now, they've added Exterminatus (horde mode), now renamed Siege, and Dreadnought Assault (team deathmatch, except kills unlock a playable dreadnought for both sides). Except, the Dreadnought Assault in SM2 is locked to Chaos exclusively (despite the fact there's a SM dreadnought model in game already). Now, why is this a problem?

Simply put, customisation. This is a minor issue in the grand scheme of things, but I'm going to add it anyway. Customisation in SM2 seems more free than SM1 on paper. In practice, however, it's incredibly restrictive, because we live in the era of microtransactions and live service BS. You need to spend in-game currency to unlock everything, including literal colours. Half of the unlocks are locked behind purely cosmetic DLC that goes for anywhere from AU$7 to AU$15, and that's always for a single chapter. Maybe if you're lucky, they'll add successors to one of the major chapters, but it's never for multiple chapters in a single pack. But it gets worse if you prefer Chaos.

See, Sabre, in their infinite wisdom, decided that Chaos didn't need customisation, because "we want all the classes to have distinct silhouettes." Yeah, funny how that was never a problem in SM1, which did have full customisation for Chaos. They finally, a good year after release, added Chaos customisation, but it's literally "do you want the Black Legion skin for your Chaos tactical, or the Black Legion Champion skin? By the way, you still need to unlock the champion variant, so get ♥♥♥♥♥♥ if you haven't played enough multiplayer to unlock it." Yeah, I doubt any Sabre employee is gonna read this, but if, by some miracle, you do, that isn't what people mean by Chaos customisation. 

And finally, Sabre is absolutely useless when it comes to fixing issues. Either they're radio silent on bugs that have been in the game since launch, or they're making the dumbest excuses to avoid having to actually do any work to fix the game. One mentioned by another reviewer - they recently added more capes, but not the ability to change the colour. Why? Because of "technical limitations." Well, technical limitations must be code for "too much work," since unpaid modders have managed to bypass this technical limitation without any issue. Or their private lobbies. Can't have more than 3 people in a squad including yourself, even when those same unpaid modders have brought the private lobby size up to 16 people, enough for a full multiplayer game. That second one is funny because, once again, Space Marine 1, a game from 2011, was able to implement full private lobbies, but a game from 2024, over a decade later, can't without the work of people who are neither paid to do it, nor part of the dev team. 

TL;DR, don't buy even on special.

I enjoy this game very much, but the new update grind is just a bridge to far for me. That's great that they want to appeal to the sweaty portion of gamers, but I do not and have not EVER enjoyed absolute difficulty at any time on this title. I am a loner as a gamer, even though I have the time to get the new ordeals...there is zero interest for me to do so. Think I'm mostly done with this title. I will probably play it a bit until I purchase Forza Horizon 6. 

Does Sabre even play this game? Part of the fun was being able to achieve some of the higher end cosmetics for lower end players. You probably just locked out 70% of your active player base, not all gamers are equal skill levels. I would argue that your player base is older and much less skilled than you assume. It was a good run on this title. 

My suggestion would be move the cosmetics requirement to Lethal. You want to have some ultra high end sweaty things, do so in limited amounts.
Another dress-up your character game. Going planet hoping just shout your cult's motto, and spread intergalactic racsm. Good game btw

I spent hours to get a prestige perk for sniper class where if I kill an enemy with 2ndary, I restore 15% of my reserve ammo. The developer has no tact to round up any calculation for perk effects or any guaranteed benefit of a perk to trigger on edge cases, that if your sniper rifle doesn't meet certain ammo threshold so you don't meet the 1.0 value in the decimals (some guns are somewhere around 0.9), you will not get the effect, at all. This is the type of minds behind the balancing and stats you have to struggle with in the vanilla game. 

They have perks with internal cooldowns where it shows none of that to you during gameplay so you have to judge the remaining cooldown by vibes, like???

Absolute idiots.

It took off the cosmetics I had already unlocked and put them behind a paywall. I enjoyed it until that happened. I really liked the gameplay but returning to find that anything I have can just be taken away and requested  to pay more for is diabolical.

I love this game, but I don't think frame generation or upscaling should be required to run more than 60fps. The framerate is real rough and hard for me to ignore when playing. I did try the upscaler and it did a whole lot of nothing while making the game look dramatically worse. I would be happy to change this review if the game gets a performance uplift.

Side note: I hate re-leveling my character for the prestige perks.

anywho the story line is flat and borring as hell, nothihng exciting, the weapons are changing betweent your choosing to the game's deafult IN EVERY CUTSCENE, no veraiety in weapons, no veraiety in enemies. games is defenetly not worth full price. 8.5 hours of campaign that took that long only because of cutscence.
this game is borring as hell. as a guy who doesnt know the format of the warhammer im in shock about this game.

The game is pretty and plays well enough.  The story is pretty good too.

Unfortunately the devs like to ignore the codex.  Your space marine's armor is basically made of cosplay foam.
Helbrutes can't warp in tabletop, why the hell do they warp in this?
Holy Jesus, why are there so many Chaos Spawn mobs?!  Even on normal difficulty expect at least 5 of these fleshy b*astards to  attempt to wear your face at once.

On the flip side, chaos marines are almost a laughable threat, when they should be a peer adversary.

Guardsmen prove to be the most badass characters in the game because they don't have cool kid space marine armor and still fight the enemy of man while facing certain death.

If you enjoy "souls like" combat, then this may be your jam.

TBH: I suck at this game and timing in melee.  I roll around, scream and shoot things.
I'm convinced the devs are masochistic xeno loving chaos worshiping heretics.

Buen juego para mamarte viendo super soldados al nivel de doom slayer partirle su madre a unos insectos 10/10, lo unico q le encuentro malo y no se si sea solo yo se crashea constantemente el juego ya trate ejecutandolo en admin o en los ajustes de windows y aun no lo resuelvo salvo eso lo recomiendo si lo puedes mover

terrible server support.

If you're planning to buy this game to play PvP, just don't.
You can't choose which server you wan't to play on, resulting in lobbies with everyone +150 ping.
i mean, if i was the problem it would be okay, but seing a complete lobby with +150 ping is just hilarious.

how can a AAA game work on a system like that?
Amazing game. Was my 2024 GOTY and I still play it to this day on pc and ps5. For sure recommend
Great game, 11 hour campaign as a tutorial for the real content

This game nuked one of my ssd's. It ran for a while after launch and everything, but something here in the last year or so messed something up.
My whole PC goes black, it went into a repair mode for my main ssd.
This happens with no other games at all, only Space Marine 2.
It was fun while it lasted, but I literally can't trust the game not to fry my computer. Be careful running this.
I started growing a beard after playing this game. I was filled with so much testosterone i almost ♥♥♥♥ my own ass.

Clunky combat, but more fluid than the first. Excellent graphics, decent plot to the single player. I think Darktide is a bit more flexible and flowing, but I have not tried the other classes yet.

It would be interesting to start seeing the other factions popping up in here

Idk much about 40k but the game is a blast. The story and writing is pretty great and the set pieces are fantastic. Pretty short - much of the value I think comes from the operations and other non-campaign elements that I've only done a little bit of so far. Not sure how that will feel in the long run but the gameplay is great so I suspect it'll be fun to play through everything.

Played the first Space Marine a decade ago. The sequel’s story is excellent, even it feels a bit short. The graphics are impressive, and the combat system is pure fun.

10/10

I usually don't leave reviews on games since everyone likes different things so my opinion is not relevant to everyone, and usually if there's something wrong with the game, it would have been mentioned already. I really do enjoy this game usually: Space Marine 2 has fun combat, most the operations are fun and the campaign is great, the story is captivating, pvp needs quite a bit of work in my opinion but there's been worse pvps in other games. 

However, the reason I write this though isn't so much about the game but rather the players you find in it. I have seen comments and reviews regarding people rushing game modes and ignoring all enemies to just finish the mission and with the recent updates especially regarding the cape grind, I can somewhat understand it as long as it is communicated amongst teammates so that is one thing; but the amount of matches I have joined/started where I have a teammate abandon the rest of us and just book it to the finish line and sit while we are stuck being absolutely battered by multiple hellbrutes and an entire Daemonic incursion or whatever enemy waves come, this behavior happens way too often. Worst part, if we die they either get wiped too since they get surprised by the teleport of a hellbrute (often the case) as they kneel in the elevator or they just leave the match instead of helping?? Might as well play with a bot that'll try and res us since they are useless and just sabotage.

Another thing you will see is teammates hogging supplies and it will always be one guy. You can usually tell if a player has played for a bit since they'll tag who should get the meds and other pick ups usually deferring to who needs it out of courtesy, but nah if you play this you will usually find in most lobbies a player who is an opportunist and will grab everything he can whenever he can even if he is full, he'll waste it just to grab the new ones even if you are in dire need of it which sucks for new players since they will never receive any resources in these medium difficulty lobbies. I have seen this so often before where one player is always grabbing supplies and the rest of us (unless healing banner is there) will stay at low health the entire game and they don't even think to leave some for you so it becomes a competition for resources and eventually you find yourself hogging in other lobbies since you are so used to having to be aggressive for it. This one is a minor issue for older players but for people getting into it, be fast and that leads to the next one:

Actually grab ♥♥♥♥ at all. I do not understand why I always see new players level 1-10 (no prestige and getting downed often and not knowing basic mechanics) playing the two hardest difficulties or even the hard stratagems and refusing to heal. I don't try to try hard but it kinda weighs down on your team if you are not helping them and are just needing to be picked up over and over during massive enemy waves or bosses and then get on the mic or type to complain about not getting picked up. In most games the difficulty is locked behind certain levels and its been a long time so maybe it is here too, but the amount of new class players I see attempting these things are out of their depth. I understand you want a challenge and I dig it, but I personally would recommend you get a trio to run with instead of subjecting public players to carrying you but maybe I'm just a hater. I try to help new players every so often so its fine for some of us but I have seen many players leave lobbies where low levels die too often or they just keep leaving when they see the level. My main issue with it though is AT LEAST HEAL. Holy, the amount of these players that REFUSE to take heals is astonishing. Relax the pride, you are not that strong. Heal brother, you definitely need it; and to the two I met that said it makes it more of a challenge and is fun, you are right about it being a challenge for us to pick you up every encounter and have no teammate for 5 minutes but it ain't fun, do that solo or with your own squad thanks. (Saber add an apothecary please)

That leaves me to my last and main issue I have been facing more recently and its not even being able to join matches. Connectivity issues has become more common but I refer to being kicked. Getting kicked after waiting a long time loading just to be loading back to the main lobby before you even get to choose your class has been a thing but now I spend hours experiencing it trying to play having that happen more often than actually playing a match. I just had it happen 5 times in a row before I started writing this and I got placed back into one of the lobbies that kicked me and I asked before they could vote why and the only reason they said was "not matching colors". Brother you could have asked me to?? That was the reason?? It has become more of an epidemic to just kick people instead of just que privately and a handful of my friends have also experienced this commonly. (Also this is not a level thing, all my classes are maxed out and even when I join one that's also maxxed out they do this). Not being able to play the game since people wanna match colors so you have to guess what people like and que wearing it ahead of time is ridiculous and should not be a thing.

Also lots of players seem to have learning disabilities or probably cannot read or listen to directions despite sounding like very much normal grown men on the mic. The amount of times me and my friends have been stuck on the Reliquary mission since we matched with randoms that do not know to match the shapes on the shrines to those on the windows despite it clearly stating it in voice and on top right is crazy and we always have to repetitively restart even after communicating properly. Just a warning.

Concluding, this community is so split in two. Half the players are some of the most most unintelligent and selfish players you will find with the exception of kids and seniors that don't understand the game just playing blissfully ignorant; and the other half are some of the kindest and funniest players you will find. The one thing that unites most players is our love of 40k, so there's that going for it. But if you plan to get this game, as of now, this is the state the game for quite a few of us and has been up until now so just be mindful. It is a great game with varying players and you will just have to roll the dice.

The campaign is fun, and there is like 12 extra missions on operations after you complete the campaign. There is a fortress/seige mode is also fun but gets boring once you play it like 10+ times. I can't say anything about PVP because you cannot select a server region and I always get matched in 200-300 ping servers, even my teammates.

The game is fun the first few times but lacks replayability
sadly didnt work but i got a re fund and im happy that i got it ilove the game demo but my computer has been getting older so it coudlt play it
This game feels like an Xbox 360 era game. A fun short campaign, co-op PvE mode, and a very casual PvP mode that isn't designed to be sweaty. It's very good

It's a great game! Only complaints are that it can feel very repetitive once you've learned the game. It's really pretty, and all the armor options look awesome.  Even running it on a gaming laptop, I get some lag, but it's a tolerable amount. On my older one I don't think I could run it period. In my opinion, it is very expensive for what it provides, so I'd watch gameplay and make sure you think you'd like it before you get it. I got it to play with my boyfriend cause we don't have any games we both play, and he loves this game.
Incredibly addictive gameplay loop, still gets free DLC updates, and is probably the best WH40K game available. A great introduction to the setting for those who are new.

its a solid game to buy on sale. If youre a big space marine fan a must but if not dont expect something that will change your life. Its a solid game to play once and a while and a good main for pve players. The PVP could use some work because its a blast but very lopsided

I have zero clue what's going on in the grand scheme of things because I don't really know Warhammer lore, but the gameplay is very fun and the game looks gorgeous. Playing co-op with my friend felt like the old Gears of War days.

I really liked playing this game, but one day, it just stopped working. I cannot get it to boot. It will launch the EasyAntiCheat splash page and then close. I have tried every tip I have been able to find. This game is fun, but it may just lock you out without notice. Buyer beware.

I was able to fix it after two weeks of fiddling with it. Still sucked that I was locked out of the game. The game is fun when it works though.

Space Marine 2 isn’t just a game. It’s a spiritual awakening. One minute you’re a normal human being with bills and responsibilities, and the next you’re screaming “FOR THE EMPRAH!” while performing a perfectly timed parry that would make Lion El Jonson shed a single, stoic tear.

The combat? Oh, it’s not just smooth. It’s smoother than a freshly polished power armor plate. You don’t fight, you waltz. A beautiful, violent ballet of bolter fire, chainsword revving, gun‑strikes, and executions so satisfying they could convert a Chaos cultist back to the light. Every encounter feels like the Emperor Himself is guiding your hand, whispering, “Smite harder, my child.”

And as you level up your class and crank up the difficulty, the game transforms from “fun” to “I have become a divine instrument of destruction.” You start making decisions like a true Astartes:

Low health? Execute something.

Surrounded? Execute everything.

Teammate down? Execute something on the way to revive them.

This game didn’t just entertain me, it dragged me headfirst into the glorious madness of Warhammer 40k. I went in knowing nothing. I came out ready to enlist, memorize the Codex Astartes, and report for duty at dawn. I now judge all problems in life by asking, “Can this be solved with a bolter?”

If you enjoy action games, co‑op chaos, or simply want to feel like a genetically engineered eight‑foot‑tall war god, I cannot recommend Space Marine 2 enough. Especially with friends. Especially if you yell battle cries. Especially if you enjoy righteous carnage.

10/10. Would purge again. For the Emperor.
I'd love to talk about the game but as it stands I can't even get into a lobby with my friends to play it.

As much as i love this game, it has many issues. its tyranid attacks are buggy and the overall combat a.i is kind of sucky. the bone warriors with whips will phase through your parries upon pariable attack and will do so several more times upon missing parry window to game bug. there are countless connection issues and im disconnected every single day every hour mid mission. as much as i love this game, its a buggy mess with more issues i have heard from the community. 10/10 game design and models, but the overall combat needs a rework or a tweak to make it less buggy and unfair towards the player
A sequel for a fan favorite game but a bit too leaning into coop and online modes in my opinion

Bought the game with 3 days early access. Been a Warhammer fan since DoW 1. SM 2 though dream come true with the brutality and gore i see in DoW 1. running on GTX 1650 4gb inside my SSD, game runs smooth ngl. Hope to get more chapters and an Apothecary class or Chaplain OR TERMINATOR class.
this game has one of the best story bro you need to get this game if you like pve and pvp and the story has ai bots if you have no friends
A nice thing to casually obliiterate heretics. Worth buying at a discounted price.

A worthy successor to Space Marine 1 and a good bit of co-op fun. That said, it's a little rough around the edges when it comes to multiplayer. Pick it up if you're keen to smash bugs and heretics.

Positives:
- regular updates and support
- interesting maps 
- core multiplayer experience is good
- great campaign

Negatives:
- online progression isn't the best, and can feel like a slog.
- gameplay loop isn't as addictive as its counterparts
- updates are very hit and miss
love this game, hoping salamanders don't get shafted with lack of content again
If you enjoy hacking, slashing, bolters, plasma incinerators and explosions (who doesn't?) you will enjoy this game.
This was my first foray into the 40k universe, and boy howdy am I hooked. This game makes you feel like a god. I don't need hard drugs when I have this.

Space Marine 2 is a game I would not have minded paying the full price for, and nowadays that should tell you A LOT about the quality of this game.

It is like playing an action movie, in the best possible way. The gameplay is really fun, the visuals are breathtaking, and the amount of love poured into every facet of this game is incredible.

While the campaign really needed three more levels to flesh out each of the characters (it was to short IMO), and the post-game multiplayer stuff comes with some really annoying design choices and a horrible UI I still do not have a grasp of, that is not enough to make me change my mind on this game being one of the best I have ever played!
Games fun, just realistically not alot of content.
Siege mode is broken, bosses keep getting bugged. I got wave 25 but it got stuck in the walls and I couldn't attack it.

I really want to like this game, I do. However, I've had issues from it from when it released; unreliable bots, buggy hit registration with Sniper and Assault, and just generally lackluster execution of ideas it seems. Take this latest update for example; the ordeals want *600* absolute runs in total just for a few extra cosmetics (that look really nice to be fair) for each class, but *why* 100 absolutes *per class* save for Techmarine 'cause he just got added? I fully understand *"In the 41st Millenium, there is only war."* but HOLY SH-T does that motif get taken to the absolute extreme. You're not rewarding your players for dedication to a grind (that we don't need ffs), you're punishing them with a slog they never asked for. 

That's not my only problem. Canonically there's like what, hundreds of Tyranid biomorphs for the 'Gaunts, Warriors, and whatever else? Why do we only get basic Termagaunts/Hormigaunts and all of 3 variations of the exact same Warrior? Where's the actual variety to the enemies? If they releaved the Pyrovore with the Biovore, that would have been cool as hell to see, an up-close in your face scuttlebug alongside the mine-seeder? Would have been amazing to see two variations of the same enemy with differing roles appearing in tandem to try and beat you down. Another problem is the difficulty, what the *hell* were you guys thinking with how difficulty scaling works? Instead of just buffing their HP, Armor, and Attack power, give us the stronger biomorphs instead, give us an actual challenge, not just a pile of code that executes super fast repeatedly and you're just meant to deal with it like a Fromsoft boss. I can go on and on and on about my own issues with the game and how many things I can think of to help solve them. 


TL;DR for those of you who hate huge walls of text for a slightly smaller one; I really want to love this game, but there are so many problems and so many odd choices made with it that I simply can't enjoy it. Too much is scuffed up between how Space Marines actually play and behave and the player experience (which is unnervingly hellacious) and the amount of tedious bullcrap one has to slog through for *cosmetics* is not something a developer should be focusing on. The player experience for casuals like myself is just simply abysmal, and before "just turn down the difficulty", difficulty is not the main problem, yes it's part of the problem, but a casual player should really not have to slog through things just to get rewarded with a few cosmetics for their tedious grind.

The game a solid 4.7/10 minimum, I know other players won't agree but I just can't keep quiet about it anymore, this is just absolutely absurd for a game developer to do to its fanbase.

Gladiator vibes, but replace the fields of flowing grass with Hormagaunts.

Trying to get parries right and some of the stun locks make me want to recite the litanies of hate though.

Good stuff, minor complaint I have to the combat system though, there is no option to have lmb be melee attack while also being the option to fire your weapon while ads as I find myself rarely hip firing, ends up needing to rebind the melee key to something like mb 4 or mb 5. If possible, I would even like it if they allow holding rmb to be ads while tapping/release rmb will perform a parry

Overall a great game, controls will take sometime to get used to but the glory one get from melee and executing heretics and xenos is unmatched, great job GWS
EULA is deranged, Easy AntiCheat in a single player part is nonsense, so I don't really know if the game is good -- the publisher thinks that we are some kind of cattle, which we are not.

Recommended

Bought this to feel like a genetically engineered demigod in power armor.

Mission accomplished.

The game’s core loop is:

1.Walk into a room.

2.Tyranids arrive in quantities normally associated with accounting errors.

3.Chainsword noises.

4.Repeat until your CPU files a complaint with the Adeptus Mechanicus.

Combat feels like someone welded Doom, Gears, and a medieval woodworking accident together. The executions are so dramatic I started apologizing to enemies before pressing the button.

Downside: after 20 hours I instinctively say “For the Emperor” every time I carry groceries.

Verdict: If you have ever looked at a horde and thought “I could fix this with violence,” this is your game.

Game's horribly unoptimized and runs terribly even on the lowest settings which is rather unusual for me as I have a pretty beefy rig but besides that, it also crashes every twenty minutes or so during missions and even while customising armour which is nuts.
Not only that but the actual customisation system (including colour pallets of all things) is almost entirely locked behind multiple paywalls.

Honestly, as a diehard fan of the first game way back in like 2011, this is extremely disappointing for me.

As of right now, I can no longer recommend the game in good faith. The actual game itself is great; however the current version is no longer playable for me. I used to have consistent performance, now after 10-20 minutes performance tanks for no reason and stays there, making it feel terrible to play. If you get it, be warned that the same could happen to you after a couple years of updates.

This game is awesome! Played it on PS5 first and logged over 200hrs, then moved to PC. I was able to move all of my content (except DLC packs) over to my PC. This game made me fall in love with the Lore so I had to go back and play Space Marine 1, and then start a real 40K army of my own for when I'm not playing on PC/

It's a damn good game. In terms of the plot, it's exactly the same as the first installment, but for me, what matters most is the audiovisual presentation and the solemn, grandiose, liturgical style of this universe, where even simple commands sound like excerpts from a sermon set against a backdrop of Gothic grandeur.

while i overall actually love the game, the most recent update just tanked performance, and caused many graphical issues without using any new equipment or armor. 

until they fix it, i would take a small break.

Very good game. Looks great, runs smooth ...

... but
- gameplay is copy-paste of industry standards (dodge and parry are horrible implemented)
- very simple level design (just forward, no side quest, no exploration at all)
- no own IP
- no innovations at all, its the same game you play for 15 years, just with new grafic.

This is a $30 game that wanna be AAA with way to high price tag and the typical cosmetic cash grab. Drip feeding content and telling you its live service :) 

The Discount price on sale (around $20) is a fair price for what you get.

Great graphics and a solid story, but the controls really threw me off. I don’t get why they didn’t stick to standard shooter controls — mouse wheel for weapon switching, right click for aiming, and not melee. Recommended, but the controls were frustrating.

Took me 8 hours to beat on the 2nd hardest difficulty and i maybe saw the final death screen 10 times? Not super difficult, but the gameplay is very fun. The presentation is some of the best there is in gaming. It was a fun time. I'll probably play some of the operations with my buddies. It's not my favorite game ever or anything, the story isn't bad but isn't terribly interesting, not much enemy variety, but overall a fun time and quick weekend game. Took probably 4 sessions to beat playing pretty casually.
This game would be fun if it works. The disconnection is scarier than any xenos or heretics. I cannot count how many times I almost finished an operation and get disconnected.

Fun game and i will finish it but the CAMPAIGN being ALWAYS online is such a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ pain. Why does my solo experience need to be on a server. I get it the moment i invite other to join the campaign but solo? Its just bad and simply for that alone i cant recommend this game. Campaigns should be able to be played offline not online

Was awesome, until it randomly decided I'm no longer allowed to play it. 
Crashes on launch from fresh install without fail, every time. Have attempted every workaround available online outside of spending MORE money to make the game playable by upgrading/changing PC parts. Their support forum has listed this issue for the last 6 months with no feedback, and I am not even CLOSE to the only one. I have people on my friends list that have had this issue happen to them as well. 
Easy Anti Cheat seems to be the most likely culprit, but then, every other game in my library that uses it, and all the ones outside my steam library that use it, will let me play them just fine. Just this one game. Until I can click play and actually play the game, I don't recommend it,
the campaign was quality but with the recent update the game crashing makes any of the online modes unplayable

download it to try the new update. they ruined siege mode, it is legitimate trash, before with no upgrades you would start struggling post wave 10 now it is still easy at wave 20,  before having 1000 points was hard even in normal, now everyone has 2000 points plus with all things unlocked and not struggling because they have added lots of defense stuff, i just am baffled, the level of stupidity has to be them trolling, is this them sabotaging the game because of no killing games because that is the only thing that makes sense to me

The game is visually stunning, runs good and's worth considering if you catch it on sale. Unfortunately, it’s weighed down by several issues. Loading times between missions are painfully long, and the PvE modes feel deserted—I rarely found other players to team up with. On the few PvE missions I did manage to join, we even got stuck because bosses failed to spawn, making progress impossible. I originally bought the game expecting something closer to Helldivers 2’s cooperative PvE experience, but it simply doesn’t deliver much on that front, it's a bit slower with far less people.

Was really looking forward to play coop. But since it's a technical desaster I'm gonna refund. No chance to play online due to the game telling us we'd be using mods. None of the workarounds you find online worked. Before that there were lots of issues to even get the game to start.
Very sad, because we really wanted to give it a try but i don't got time for such a mess.

Could not play this game for a single minute, keeps crashing and freezing, I didn't receive any support on this matter either, not from the devs or the community.  Tried everything I could think of and read about. I have never rated a game this low, but 0/10

This game is pretty cool, coming from someone who knows nothing about 40K prior to playing this game, this is a good introduction to 40K. If you like games with campaign/mission games like maybe Outlast Trials, you might like this too.
Just started to play game. Seems okay, got to get a handle on playing...

A very cool game, especially for people who don't understand anything about WH40k's lore like me. Interesting cooperative gameplay, side missions are fun to level up classes. A game about tough guys who run around, kill everyone, and shout, “For the Emperor!”

Graphically impressive, but the story is a let down and the gameplay is pretty fun but never amazing. It's a by the numbers copy-paste 40K story that the die hard fans will like simply for the opportunity to be immersed in the detailed 40K world, but if you're an older fan you'll likely be left disappointed and wanting more than what's here.

The weapons seem poorly balanced, and don't reflect the feeling of power that they should evoke. There's barely any difference between a lot of the special bolters and most feel weak. Monsters are hit point sponges, and the controls aren't as responsive as they should be, even on a good gaming rig. Get it on sale if you're still keen.
do not buy it for online gaming. a lot of server and connection problems... (Error Code : 4 Even in June 2026)
ABSOLUTE CINEMA, wow what a great game. Can't wait for the next game
